Business banking /
Payment solutions /
Solutions by industry

Payment solutions by industry

Powerful payment
solutions for every
industry

Whether you own a retail store, run a restaurant, provide special services or operate on the go, U.S. Bank’s point-of-sale (POS) and payment solutions are built to empower your business and streamline operations.

Elevate your business with a point-of-sale solution designed for you.

eCommerce

Create and manage a professional online storefront with integrations from U.S. Bank.

  • Launch your online store with simple setup and flexible checkout options for your customers.

  • Scale your business with features designed to support everyday operations and overall business growth.

  • Gain quick access to your funds and enjoy 24/7 support to ensure your business runs accordingly.

Learn more

Restaurants

Pair your passion with a POS system from U.S. Bank.

  • Manage tables, fire tickets by course and split checks by position.

  • Customize your menu and offer contactless ordering options online or in-person.

  • Make closing time a breeze with real-time reporting on revenues, payments and tips. 

Learn more

Retail

Embrace a POS solution that’s a one-stop shop for managing your retail business.

  • Accept all major credit cards and contactless payments, including Apple Pay™.

  • Simplify returns with support for refunds, exchanges or store credit.

  • Manage inventory and get insights into your top selling products with real-time reports.

Learn more

Services

Deliver top-tier customer experiences with a POS solution built to serve your needs.

  • Use appointment booking to manage customer services and employee schedules.

  • Offer more ways for customers to pay with cash, card and contactless methods.

  • Keep track of customer activity and reward loyalty with special services or discounts.

Learn more

A funding alternative that gives you peace-of-mind.

U.S. Bank payment processing customers can unlock fast, flexible access to cash capital with Quick Capital1—up to $500,000, advanced against future sales. To learn more details, visit Payments Insider.

Special features for businesses like yours

Our POS systems do more than accept payments. No matter which solution or plan you choose, we offer features to customize and optimize your business operations.

Invoicing

Generate new invoices and send them directly to customers to pay online. Set the terms of payment and if it becomes overdue, our invoicing software can send automatic reminders.

Learn more

Appointment booking

Drive sales and improve staff efficiency with online appointment booking. Customers can book appointments from your website, and your team can view appointments in real time. Create, confirm, change or cancel appointments with ease.

Learn more

Online Ordering

Increase revenue with Online Ordering for restaurant and retail businesses. Set up your own mobile-friendly online ordering site in minutes, showcase your products and start taking orders right away.

Learn more

Talk to a payments expert specializing in your industry.

Our industry-leading payments experts will work with you to tailor payment solutions and services specifically tailored to your needs.

Speak with a payments expert directly at 844-245-0102 or request a call for more information.

Request a call

Frequently asked questions

Point-of-sale (POS) systems are widely used across various industries to streamline the process of conducting sales transactions, manage inventory and improve overall business efficiency. Some industries where POS systems are commonly used include retail, restaurants, hospitality and lodging, entertainment, healthcare, salons and spas, service-based businesses, ecommerce, automotive and more.

Deciding to use a POS system depends on a few factors. In general, if you take payments, you should use a POS system. POS systems are particularly beneficial for retail businesses, restaurants and service providers that deal with a high volume of transactions and require features like inventory management, sales tracking and reporting. Even mobile businesses like food trucks or farmers’ market vendors can benefit from a POS system. U.S. Bank offers several POS plans for all types of businesses – from mobile POS plans up to full-service POS systems.

Point-of-sale systems work by facilitating and automating the various tasks involved in taking payments. The process typically involves several steps, including product database setup, (each item in inventory receives a unique identifier, such as a barcode, and includes information like price and description), item scanning, price calculation, payment processing, receipt generation, inventory management. reporting and analytics, and security.

POS systems can vary in terms of features and capabilities. Some businesses may use basic or free POS systems that handle essential functions, while others may opt for more advanced systems with additional features like customer relationship management, loyalty programs and online integration. The goal of a POS system is to streamline and enhance the efficiency of the sales process while providing businesses with valuable insights into their operations.
